DBA FOODS CO.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE SET FOR FEB-25-2014 CONTINUED TO APR-15-2014 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610 FOR FAILURE TO FILE PROOF OF SERVICE AND OBTAIN ANSWER(S) FROM, OR ENTER DEFAULT(S) AGAINST, OR DISMISS DEFENDANT(S) FOOD4LESS AND RALPHS GROCERY COMPANY AS TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. ***NOTE: IF THERE IS NO PROOF OF SERVICE, ANSWER, DEFAULT OR DISMISSAL OF DEFENDANTS FOOD4LESS AND RALPHS GROCERY COMPANY AS TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT BY THE APR-15-2014 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE HEARING, AND NO APPEARANCE BY PLAINTIFF, THE COURT INTENDS TO DISMISS THESE DEFENDANTS. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
ORDER ON DEFENDANT CALA FOODS, INC. DBA FOODS CO.'S MOTION TO HAVE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION DEEMED ADMITTED AND FOR THE IMPOSITION OF MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF.

DISCOVERY 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CALA FOODS, INC. DBA FOODS CO.'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS ESTABLISHED THAT PLAINTIFF MARITZA HURTADO WAS AWARE OF THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, PROMISED RESPONSES ON A 10 DAY EXTENSION OFFERED BY DEFENDANT AND FAILED TO PROVIDE RESPONSES WITHIN THE 10 DAY EXTENSION OR ANYTIME THEREAFTER. PLAINTIFF IS HEREBY ORDERED TO PROVIDE VERIFIED RESPONSES WITHOUT OBJECTIONS TO DEFENDANT WITHIN 20 DAYS OF SERVICE OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THE ORDER ON THIS MOTION. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF IS WARNED THAT FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THIS ORDER MAY SUBJECT HER TO TERMINATING SANCTIONS UPON APPLICATION BY DEFENDANT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM PETER J. VAN ZANDT, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CALA FOODS, INC. DBA FOODS CO.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE WILLFUL FAILURE TO WARN COUNT UNDER CIVIL CODE 846, WHICH IS INAPPLICABLE SINCE IT PERTAINS TO RECREATIONAL PURPOSES. THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED AS TO THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS. PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES THAT DEFENDANT ACTED WITH A CONSCIOUS DISREGARD FOR THE SAFETY OF OTHERS. SEE TAYLOR V. SUP. CRT. (1979) 24 CAL.3D 890, 895-896. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976

DISCOVERY 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CALA FOODS, INC. DBA FOODS CO.'S MOTION TO HAVE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS DEEMED ADMITTED IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS ESTABLISHED THAT PLAINTIFF MARITZA HURTADO WAS AWARE OF THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS, PROMISED RESPONSES ON A 10 DAY EXTENSION OFFERED BY DEFENDANT AND FAILED TO PROVIDE RESPONSES WITHIN THE 10 DAY EXTENSION OR ANYTIME THEREAFTER. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF MUST PAY TO DEFENDANT THE SUM OF $500.00 WITHIN 20 DAYS OF SERVICE OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THE ORDER ON THIS MOTION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM PETER J. VAN ZANDT, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Cala Foods, Inc., Dba Foods Co.'S To Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted And For The Imposition Of Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff
Off Calendar Mar-06-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: TEO ALDRICH, ESQ., 925.280.0004, FOR DEFENDANT CALA FOODS, INC. DBA FOODS CO.; NO OTHER APPEARANCES.

RULING: THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. Defendant Cala Foods, Inc. dba Foods Co.'s motion to have Requests for Admissions deemed admitted is GRANTED. Defendant has established that plaintiff Maritza Hurtado was aware of the Requests for Admissions, promised responses on a 10 day extension offered by Defendant and failed to provide responses within the 10 day extension or anytime thereafter. Defendant's request for sanctions is GRANTED. Plaintiff must pay to Defendant the sum of $500.00 within 20 days of service of notice of entry of the order on this motion. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM PETER J. VAN ZANDT, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Cala Foods, Inc., Dba Foods Co., To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And For The Imposition Of Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff
Off Calendar Mar-06-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: TEO ALDRICH, ESQ., 925.280.0004, FOR DEFENDANT CALA FOODS, INC. DBA FOODS CO.; NO OTHER APPEARANCES.

RULING: THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. Defendant Cala Foods, Inc. dba Foods Co.'s motion to Compel Responses to Special Interrogatories is GRANTED. Defendant has established that plaintiff Maritza Hurtado was aware of the Special Interrogatories, promised responses on a 10 day extension offered by Defendant and failed to provide responses within the 10 day extension or anytime thereafter. Plaintiff is hereby ORDERED to provide verified responses without objections to Defendant within 20 days of service of notice of entry of the order on this motion. Defendant's request for monetary sanctions is DENIED. Plaintiff is warned that failure to comply with this Order may subject her to terminating sanctions upon application by Defendant. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM PETER J. VAN ZANDT, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: VERONICA H. GARCIA, ESQ., 510.663.3700, FOR PLAINTIFF MARITZAH HURTADO.
TED ALDRICH, ESQ., 925.274.9500, FOR DEFENDANT CALA FOODS, INC. DBA FOODS CO.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT CALA FOODS, INC. DBA FOODS CO.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is granted without leave to amend as to the willful failure to warn count under Civil Code 846, which is inapplicable since it pertains to recreational purposes. The motion to strike is denied as to the punitive damages allegations. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges that defendant acted with a conscious disregard for the safety of others. See Taylor v. Sup. Crt. (1979) 24 Cal.3d 890, 895-896. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
